name: Avaza Project Management API
what_it_does: All-in-one project management platform for service businesses with REST API — combining tasks, time tracking, expenses, resource scheduling, and invoicing with chat, offering a comprehensive API for CRM and accounting system integration.
use_cases: Creating projects and tasks from CRM via API for service team workflow
Querying tracked time and expenses for automated invoice generation
Syncing Avaza resource schedules with external capacity planning tools
Exporting project financial data to accounting systems via API
Monitoring project profitability by querying time and budget data
not_for: Large enterprise portfolio management (use Jira or MS Project)
best_when: A service business or agency needs an all-in-one project + time + invoicing tool with API access for integration with CRM and accounting systems.
avoid_when: You need enterprise features, complex project portfolio management, or non-service business workflows.

auth.notes: OAuth2 Authorization Code flow for third-party integrations. Bearer token auth. No fine-grained scopes — full account access. API documentation available at Swagger endpoint. Token expiry requires refresh implementation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 5 users, 5 projects, 5GB storage
pricing.paid_tiers: Startup from $11.95/month; Basic from $23.95/month; Business from $47.95/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Generous free tier with 5 users. Flat-rate pricing (not per-user) for paid plans — good value for growing teams. Australian company. API on all paid plans. Strong Zapier integration. Comprehensive feature set for service businesses. Backed by Atlassian alumni.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply; documented in API reference

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with Swagger documentation for projects, tasks, time entries, expenses, invoices, and users. OAuth2 for integrations. No webhooks — polling required. Flat-rate pricing model unusual — API available on paid plans regardless of user count.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Time entry creation not idempotent — creates new records. Project management idempotent by ID. Invoice creation not idempotent. Read operations idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: No webhooks — must poll for data changes
Flat-rate pricing means API access not tied to seat count
Chat messages are a separate entity from task comments
Resource scheduling is a distinct module from project tasks

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. Australian company. AU/US data residency. OAuth2 for integrations. No scope granularity. Standard SaaS security for SMB project tool.
